exemplar.title: How does F. Scott Fitzgerald employ symbols in The Great Gatsby to accentuate the fragmentation of identity in the face of urbanisation in the Roaring Twenties?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does F. Scott Fitzgerald employ symbols in The Great Gatsby to accentuate the fragmentation of identity in the face of urbanisation in the Roaring Twenties?

subject.title: English Lang & Lit (Old)
subject.description: IB English Language & Literature combines the study of language and literature, analyzing both literary and non-literary texts across various media and contexts. Students explore how language shapes meaning, identity, and culture through critical examination of diverse text types including advertisements, speeches, novels, and digital media. The course develops analytical skills, cultural understanding, and critical thinking about communication in different contexts and purposes.

strengths: Accurate and sustained interpretation of key symbols linked to the historical context
Consistent use of relevant quotations to support main points
Clear demonstration of understanding of how motifs inform the theme of identity fragmentation
weaknesses: Occasional factual slips (e.g., misspelling âEckleburg,â mischaracterising Myrtleâs motives)
Uneven citation practice and incomplete bibliographic details
Limited engagement with more complex narrative devices (e.g., unreliable narration, irony)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the analysis remains at a generally âsatisfactoryâ level in places, the candidate consistently integrates textual references and sustains an interpretation that convincingly links symbols to thematic implications. These strengths justify a high band-5 allocation for knowledge and understanding, reflecting the examinerâs overall mark of 17/20 and the studentâs strong performance in this criterion.

strengths: Appropriate identification of symbolism, juxtaposition, and imagery
Good moments of insight (e.g., decay imagery in the garden)
Effective use of quotations such as âwhite palaces glittered along the waterâ to illustrate social critique
weaknesses: Analysis occasionally drifts into thematic summary rather than close examination of craft
Technical terms and deeper evaluation of literary devices (e.g., irony, allusions) are limited
Some points are asserted rather than argued, reducing analytical depth
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The candidate delivers generally appropriate and at times insightful analysis that moves beyond mere description, meriting band-4. While richer technical discussion and more consistent evaluation would be needed for the top band, the overall strength of interpretation and evidence use aligns with a score of 4 in this criterion.

strengths: Clear overall structure with a coherent introduction, symbol-focused body paragraphs, and conclusion
Well-articulated line of inquiry that guides the reader through the argument
Most quotations are integrated into the prose
weaknesses: Digressions into historical background disrupt the line of inquiry
Paragraph splits mid-sentence and occasional repetition weaken cohesion
Conclusion reiterates rather than synthesizes critical findings
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay is well organized and mostly cohesive, with an adequately developed line of inquiry and generally competent integration of examples. Minor structural lapses and uneven transitions keep it below the top band, justifying a band-4 score for focus, organization, and development.

strengths: Language is generally clear and readable with varied sentence structures
Formal academic register is consistently maintained
Good command of vocabulary to discuss literary concepts
weaknesses: Recurring errors in spelling (âEckleburg,â âEvesâ) and agreement slips interrupt fluency
Several overlong or convoluted sentences and inconsistent punctuation around citations
Occasional awkward phrasing and tense inconsistencies detract from stylistic polish
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The candidate writes with clear and accurate language overall, maintaining an appropriate register. Despite some lapses in spelling, syntax, and punctuation, the level of clarity and control warrants a band-4 score in language rather than the lower band indicated by isolated errors.

strandSelections.reasoning: Most quotations are embedded in the prose, but punctuation and syntactic integration are uneven; for instance, commas are frequently misplaced around parenthetical references and some quotes sit awkwardly at sentence ends.  Commentary usually follows the evidence, yet at times quotations are left to âspeak for themselves.â  Integration is therefore competent but not mostly well-integrated, meriting a mid-band placement.

The student summarises the plot accurately and links the three selected symbols to the historical context of 1920s urbanisation, showing a satisfactory grasp of how each motif feeds the theme of fragmented identity.  However, the discussion stays at a fairly general level; complexities such as Nickâs unreliable narration or Fitzgeraldâs irony are not addressed, and a few factual slips (e.g. misspelling Eckleburg, implying Myrtle ârespects her marriageâ) weaken authority.  Overall, the interpretation is sustained but not consistently probing or persuasive, aligning with a satisfactoryârather than goodâlevel of knowledge and understanding.

The structure is clear: introduction, three body sections organised around symbols, and a conclusion.  Topic sentences and a stated line of inquiry guide the reader, yet cohesion is weakened by digressions into historical background, some paragraph splits mid-sentence, and occasional repetition.  Development of the central argument is adequate but not consistently logical or progressive, suiting the descriptor for adequate organisation.

Quotations are supplied for every main point and are generally relevant (e.g. âashes grow like wheat,â âmoths among the whisperingsâ).  Yet citation practice is unevenâsome page numbers are missing, several quotations are truncated or inaccurately copied (âEves,â âEkcleburgâ), and secondary sources are mentioned in passing but not integrated analytically.  The references mostly support the argument but are not chosen or deployed with the precision expected for higher bands.

The student maintains an appropriately formal, academic register for an HL literary essay and avoids colloquialisms.  However, occasional awkward phrasing ("lick [her] shoe" explanatory insertion, parenthetical source notes mid-sentence) and inconsistent verb tenses detract from stylistic polish.  Hence register and style are mostly appropriate but not consistently controlled or effective, aligning with band 3.

The language is generally clear and readable, with academic vocabulary and varied sentence structures.  Still, recurring errorsâmisspellings ("Eves", "Ekcleburg"), agreement slips, and some convoluted or run-on sentencesâinterrupt fluency.  Such lapses fit the description of âadequate accuracy despite some lapsesâ rather than the higher band of âgood accuracy.â

The essay moves beyond description by identifying symbolism, juxtaposition and imagery, and by linking these devices to social critique.  Nevertheless, the commentary often drifts into thematic summary (âurbanisation fosters false superiorityâ) rather than close examination of Fitzgeraldâs craft; technical terms are limited and several points are asserted rather than evaluated.  Insightful moments appear (e.g. âgarden twisted into decayâ), but they are intermittent, placing the work firmly in the âgenerally appropriate analysisâ band.

annotations.comment: The line of inquiry is clearly articulated, linking symbols to identity fragmentation and urbanisation. This focus provides a coherent roadmap for the essayâs argument.
The phrasing of the inquiry is wordy; consider simplifying for precision (e.g., define âfragmentation of identityâ more succinctly).
The historical overview of the Roaring Twenties relies heavily on tertiary sources and remains too general; focus the context on urbanisationâs specific impact as portrayed in the novel.
The paragraph devotes excessive space to plot summary; condense narrative description and allocate more analysis to symbol interpretation.
Citation practice is uneven; some quotations lack page numbers and others use inconsistent parenthetical formatting. Establish a uniform in-text referencing style.
The stray math notation at the paragraphâs end is an artifact; remove or clarify its relevance as it disrupts flow.
The claim that Myrtle ârespects her marriageâ contradicts the text; she flagrantly engages in an affair. Reassess this interpretation to maintain textual accuracy.
Several sentences are overlong and convoluted; break them into shorter, clearer statements to improve readability.
The phrase âemphasises on the moral decayâ is grammatically incorrect; remove âonâ to correct register (âemphasises the moral decayâ).
Several paragraphs split mid-thought, which disrupts cohesion. Ensure each paragraph contains a complete idea or analytical unit.
The analysis of East vs. West Egg would benefit from closer attention to Fitzgeraldâs diction and irony; unpack terms like âwhite palacesâ and âmothsâ in context.
Effectively chosen quote: âwhite palaces glittered along the waterâ conveys the unattainable glamour of old money. Good use of imagery.
You reference Gatsbyâs âPlatonic conception of himselfâ but do not analyse the allusion to Plato; explore this philosophical layer to deepen your evaluation.
Punctuation after parenthetical citations is inconsistent (e.g., missing periods). Standardize citation punctuation to improve accuracy.
The discussion of T.J. Eckleburg repeats âerosion of spiritual valuesâ without advancing insight; vary phrasing and develop new angles.
The misspelling âEvesâ and âEkcleburgâ appears multiple times; ensure accurate spelling of key terms to maintain authority.
Analysis often trails off after quoting; follow each quotation with specific commentary on Fitzgeraldâs technique.
Phrase âevinced by the judgemental toneâ is awkward; consider rephrasing for clarity (e.g., âunderscored by the eyesâ judgmental gazeâ).
The conclusion reiterates summary points rather than synthesizing the argument; deepen the final reflection to emphasize your critical findings.
âEkcleburgâ is misspelled in the conclusion again; correct spelling to âEckleburgâ for consistency.
This sentence merges references to Myrtle and the novelâs overall theme without clarity; split into separate sentences to maintain focus.
The final paragraph contains run-on sentences that obscure argument flow; break complex sentences for precision.
The conclusion misses an opportunity to integrate Nickâs narrative perspective as a lens of hope amid fragmentation; consider referencing his role explicitly.
Reference list formatting is inconsistent (e.g., missing italics, variable date formats). Adopt a uniform citation style such as MLA or APA.
Several secondary sources appear in the bibliography but are barely integrated in the essay; ensure each reference is clearly connected to your analysis.
The Fitzgerald novel entry lacks full publisher details (city, edition); include complete publication information to strengthen academic rigor.
